content.content:
# c-moji050w-ea9fa351fb3c [live]
> Cartograph + surveying cascade — patron-vision authoring on disk through recursive surveying to mandate dispatch. Settled architecture lives at /workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md; sanctum-side planning at .scratch/cartograph-design-synthesis.md. Subsumes the c-moa42rxh vision-keeper subtree (linked supersedes).
Status: live
Supersedes: c-moa42rxh "Design the vision-keeper role — the agent/apparatus that holds a forward-looking product vision and decomposes it into commissionable work"
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:23.120Z

## c-moje4foc-bdbf4fc19e7e [parked]
> When multiple surveyor implementations are registered with the substrate, how does the substrate select which one to dispatch for a given survey petition? Per-vision config (each vision declares its preferred surveyor)? Per-cartograph-tree (one surveyor per product)? Round-robin? Default-with-override mechanism? Not for v0 (only one surveyor registered) but worth not forgetting.
Status: parked
Created: 2026-04-29T01:42:45.084Z

## c-moji0bdf-3eb8860ca55b [concluded]
> Commission A — Vision authoring on disk + apply CLI. nsg vision apply CLI verb (single code path for first-import and Nth re-import via visionId binding in sidecar), priority-flag CLI surface (--severity --deadline --decay), writes to ext['surveyor']. Ships in cartograph-apparatus. Single-event-per-apply guarantee: createVision must accept initial stage OR apply wraps create+transition in one transaction (decided during spec). Arch §3.1.
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Dispatched as commission w-moji5a2z-2c64c95ac846 (codex nexus, posted directly as open). Brief covers nsg vision apply CLI: single code path for first/Nth import via visionId in sidecar, --severity/--deadline/--decay flags writing to ext['surveyor'], single-event-per-apply guarantee (implementer chooses between createVision-with-stage param or transactional wrap), one-way file→writ data flow, stale-binding warn+error. Open sub-clicks (stage gating c-moji0zzi, sidecar layout c-moji13ti) remain live to track post-commission outcomes since the brief carries v0 leans without foreclosing later revision.
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:31.347Z
Resolved: 2026-04-29T03:36:40.667Z

### c-moji0zzi-968aa0d2e875 [live]
> Stage gating policy — should drafts trigger surveys, or only stage:active? v0 lean: every nsg vision apply triggers surveying. Layer in stage-gating later if draft-without-surveying becomes a real workflow need. Risk: patron iterating with multiple applies during initial authoring accumulates survey writs. Possible mitigation: substrate-side debounce. Arch §5.1.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:03.246Z

### c-moji13ti-43be2761a57a [live]
> Sidecar file organization — system fields (visionId, written by apply CLI) mixed with patron fields (stage, codex, severity, deadline). Editing risks accidentally clobbering visionId; git-blame mixes system + patron history. v0 lean: keep merged sidecar; document visionId as system-managed not-edited-by-hand. Cleanup option later: split into hidden system file (.vision-binding.yml) + patron metadata file. Decide during commission A. Arch §5.13.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:08.214Z

## c-moji0d5n-f6ac240fd965 [concluded]
> Commission B — Delete @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus plugin (Reckoner-contract worked example; naming-collision landmine with the redefined Surveyor) and update framework architecture docs to redefine The Surveyor as the cartograph-decomposition apparatus. Sanctum-coordinated framework change. Lands first to clear the path for substrate work.
Status: concluded
Conclusion: Dispatched as commission w-moji63xm-9ebd9a8a302c (codex nexus, posted directly as open). Brief covers two-part cleanup: (1) delete @shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus plugin entirely (Reckoner-contract worked example; naming-collision landmine); rewrite petitioner-registration.md worked-example sections to stand without the in-tree example; (2) redefine The Surveyor in guild-metaphor.md, architecture/index.md, and architecture/plugins.md from codex-awareness to cartograph-decomposition apparatus, per the new arch doc at docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md.
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:33.659Z
Resolved: 2026-04-29T03:36:43.500Z

## c-moji0ggh-19021cbf9782 [live]
> Commission C — Surveyor-apparatus substrate plugin. Owns survey writ types (survey-vision/charge/piece), books.surveys, surveyor registry (kit-contribution surface), CDC observer, ext['surveyor'] slot + dimension-translation logic, rig-template routing, SurveyDoc stamping, and the surveyor anima tool surface (create_charge[s], create_piece[s], create_mandate[s]). Mirrors Reckoner substrate+extension+default pattern. Arch §3.2-§3.10. Needs design resolution on dynamic rig templates (§5.2), writ type shape (§5.3), substrate scope (§5.6), rig failure (§5.10), zero-children (§5.14) before brief drafts.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:37.937Z

### c-moje49pk-d8c4f069656a [live]
> What exactly does the surveyor-apparatus substrate own vs leave to surveyor implementations? Candidates for substrate: CDC observer for cartograph node creation, initial survey petition emission, rig-template routing, SurveyDoc creation+stamping, follow-up petition posting on child cartograph nodes, default priority-dimension scheme, role-file convention. Candidates for surveyor-implementation: rig templates (with reasoning prompts), priority dimension preferences, decomposition heuristics, stop conditions, source-id grammar. Goal: push as much as possible into substrate so surveyor implementations can be minimal kit contributions. Affects c-moiu8pm9 (initial petition timing) and c-moiu8tm4 (periodic re-evaluation).
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T01:42:37.352Z

### c-moji16hw-87571df7bbcc [live]
> Survey writ type shape — three layer-specific types (survey-vision / survey-charge / survey-piece) or one survey type with targetType field. v0 lean: three (matches cartograph's per-layer types; Spider's static-config dispatch is direct). One-type-with-field is a revisit option if dynamic-rig-template work makes it cheaper. Arch §5.3.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:11.684Z

### c-moji19qa-76f1c91e6445 [live]
> Cascading supersedes policy — when a charge ch-3 is superseded by ch-3-new, what happens to ch-3's pieces? Substrate default (lean for v0): pieces stay attached to superseded parent; their lifecycle continues independently. Patron walkthrough surfaces orphaned children for resolution. Patron ergonomics caveat: a 'soft cancel' CLI surface (nsg charge cancel ch-3 --cascade) may be earlier than v0+1 to handle this cleanly. Arch §5.8.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:15.874Z

### c-moji1cff-ff7619f0936b [live]
> Survey rig failure handling — does Spider's mandate-retry semantics compose with survey writ types? Lean: yes, Spider's existing retry semantics apply uniformly across dispatchable types — survey writs get the same retry treatment as mandates. After max retries the survey writ moves to failed phase; the cartograph node has no children for that round; patron sees a stuck-decomposition signal. Confirm during substrate commission audit. Arch §5.10.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:19.371Z

### c-moji1fpx-fb19a2cc74dc [live]
> Zero-children survey rig behavior — what if a survey rig completes with no children created (rig decides target node is too vague, or sufficient as-is)? Substrate has no special handling: survey writ terminates with notes-only and the cartograph node sits without further decomposition. Lean: zero-children is a valid rig outcome; notes carry the explanation; patron sees 'surveyed but no children' via dashboard. Confirm during scaffold-surveyor commission whether prompt should encourage rigs to produce *something* vs accept zero. Arch §5.14.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:23.637Z

## c-moji0i7s-990036e0e009 [live]
> Commission D — Spider rig-resolution audit + dispatch extension for survey writ types. Audit Spider's current rig-resolution mechanism (how it discriminates rigs across writ types/kinds), extend dispatchable-type set to include survey-vision/charge/piece, integrate template lookup with the substrate's surveyor registry. May fold into Commission C or stand alone depending on audit scope.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:40.217Z

### c-moje41iq-5c1496c71378 [live]
> How do plugin-contributed rig templates reach Spider for dispatch? Currently rigs are statically configured by writ type via Spider's rigTemplateMappings. Surveyor implementations need to register rig templates (survey-vision/charge/piece) at plugin load time. Options enumerated: (1) dynamic kit-contribution registration (matches Reckoner scheduler pattern — strongest fit), (2) static config with templating (poor fit; surveyors want fundamentally different rigs not parameter variations), (3) rig 'include' composition primitive (orthogonal — useful for shared substrate boilerplate but doesn't solve registration), (4) engine grafting/graftTailing (defensible alternative — substrate provides one universal survey rig, surveyors contribute engines that get grafted at runtime; higher substrate share but more complex). Prerequisite audit: how does Spider currently discriminate between rigs when multiple kinds of mandate exist (implementer/spec/reviewer/surveyor) — by codex, ext field, rig name? Cross-references c-mo1mqfsa rig-template subtree (patch composition c-mo3ibgoe, Fabricator-driven construction c-mo3ibgzt).
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T01:42:26.738Z

## c-moji0km6-21d100a63504 [live]
> Commission E — scaffold-surveyor default plugin. New @shardworks/scaffold-surveyor with minimal LLM-driven rig templates per cartograph layer (vision/charge/piece). Registers via kit contribution. First concrete surveyor; designed to be replaced as surveyor approaches are iterated. End-to-end smoke test: vision applied → survey writ created → rig dispatched → charges created → recursion fires → mandates created. Depends on C + D. First-light commission for the whole cascade.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:43.326Z

### c-moje4dj9-d3cabbdb0e6c [live]
> Design the default scaffold-surveyor — first concrete surveyor implementation. Provides minimal rig templates for survey-vision/charge/piece (basic LLM prompts asking the rig to read parent, decompose into N children with reasoning, fill SurveyDoc.notes). Useful immediately for getting first surveys flowing; designed to be replaced as surveyor approaches are iterated. Open: model selection, prompt structure, stop conditions (when to stop creating children vs decompose further), how to handle re-survey (drift detection re-runs), whether one recursive rig template or three layer-specific templates.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T01:42:42.309Z

### c-moji1jb9-bb798ad1a17a [live]
> Mandate-creation priority handoff — when a surveyor rig at the piece (or charge) layer creates a leaf mandate, it stamps ext['reckoner'] directly via reckoner.petition stamp-only form. No surveying-of-mandates. Discipline question: how does the rig choose Reckoner dimensions? Currently no constraint mechanism — rig could over-claim (everything serious) and starve other guild work. Operational tuning rather than substrate mechanism for v0; revisit if observed drift. Arch §5.5.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:32:28.293Z

## c-moji0maw-134717b51aa7 [live]
> Commission F — Vision completion criteria. Design what 'vision complete' means and the CLI/UX for getting there. Current lean (arch §5.9): patron-explicit transition, no auto-completion. Cases to handle: some-validated-some-dropped, partial walkthrough, sunset with in-flight work. Independent of substrate.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:45.512Z

## c-moji0nti-3930fe2f35cd [live]
> Commission G — Patron walkthrough CLI surface. The patron-contract validation step at the charge layer. Substantial design problem (arch §5.11): how delivered work is presented to the patron, what the validation interaction looks like, how feedback feeds back into the vision file, transition handling. Half of the patron's value lives here. Independent of substrate work.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:47.478Z

## c-moji0q4y-f0463b656949 [live]
> Documentation: re-survey upstream-cascade behavior. The substrate's CDC observer fires on changes to the node being decomposed, NOT upstream of it — so vision edits don't auto-cascade re-surveys to children whose content didn't change. Structurally correct (immutability) but may surprise patrons. Real remediation: patron edits the vision in a way that forces the upstream rig to supersede the charge. Document the behavior; not a code change. Arch §5.15.
Status: live
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:50.483Z

## c-moji0rrl-eacd7a1f9c41 [parked]
> Future: per-charge priority nudge CLI. Patron sets --severity at vision-apply time; no per-charge nudge CLI surface. Heavy workaround today is re-edit vision and re-apply (re-fires surveys). Future: small nsg charge nudge ch-2 --severity serious CLI is cheap to add later. Defer until needed. Arch §5.12.
Status: parked
Created: 2026-04-29T03:31:52.593Z

content.content:
1	# Cartograph + Surveying Cascade — Planning & Commission Synthesis
2	
3	> Sanctum-side planning artifact. The settled architecture lives in
4	> the framework repo at
5	> [`docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md`](/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md).
6	> This doc holds the planning structure, phased commissions, click
7	> tree, and source-click traceability. Read the arch doc first if
8	> you want the design itself.
9	
10	---
11	
12	## 1. State of the system
13	
14	### 1.1 What's shipped
15	
16	| Component | Package | Key commits | Status |
17	|---|---|---|---|
18	| Cartograph data layer (vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs) | `@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus` | `f69f1c6` (scaffold) | **Shipped** |
19	| Cartograph CLI (vision/charge/piece × create/show/list/patch/transition) | (in cartograph-apparatus) | `a04cd6c` | **Shipped** |
20	| Reckoner core (registry, source-id grammar, ext slot, petition/withdraw helpers) | `@shardworks/reckoner-apparatus` | `89fb1ab`, `64113e0`, `f7fb5b0` | **Shipped** |
21	| Reckoner CDC handler + Reckonings book + startup catch-up | (reckoner) | `e59abd6` | **Shipped** |
22	| Reckoner scheduler kit-contribution (always-approve default) | (reckoner) | `d068b65` | **Shipped** |
23	| Reckoner periodic tick (replaces CDC handler) | (reckoner) | `7bed456` | **Shipped** |
24	| Reckoner dependency-aware consideration gate | (reckoner) | `0e1e81f` | **Shipped** |
25	| `depends-on` link kind (Clerk-contributed) | clerk + spider | `5f4a0f1` | **Shipped** |
26	| Vision-keeper worked-example petitioner | `@shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus` | `4147d4f`, `b92dc90` | **To delete (Commission B)** |
27	
28	### 1.2 What's in flight
29	
30	Nothing currently in flight in this domain.
31	
32	### 1.3 What hasn't been built
33	
34	The entire surveying cascade machinery:
35	
36	- Disk-authored visions (`<GUILD>/vision/<slug>/vision.md` + sidecar)
37	- `nsg vision apply` CLI verb (and its priority flags)
38	- Survey writ types (`survey-vision` / `survey-charge` / `survey-piece`)
39	- SurveyDoc companion type and `books.surveys`
40	- `ext['surveyor']` slot — priority hints
41	- Surveyor-apparatus substrate plugin
42	- Default scaffold-surveyor plugin
43	- Spider extension: dispatch survey-* writ types in addition to mandates
44	
45	---
46	
47	## 2. Commission readiness — what can ship now vs what needs more design
48	
49	### 2.1 Readiness summary
50	
51	| Commission | Status | Gating open questions |
52	|---|---|---|
53	| **B** Cleanup (delete vision-keeper plugin + redefine The Surveyor) | **Ready now** | None — fully settled |
54	| **A** Vision authoring on disk + apply CLI | **Ready now** (with v0 leans) | Stage gating (arch §5.1; v0 lean: every apply triggers); sidecar layout (arch §5.13; v0 lean: keep merged) |
55	| **C** Surveyor-apparatus substrate | **Needs design first** | Dynamic rig templates (arch §5.2); writ type shape 1 vs 3 (§5.3); substrate scope (§5.6); rig failure handling (§5.10); zero-children behavior (§5.14) |
56	| **D** Spider rig-resolution audit + dispatch extension | **Needs design first** | Spider audit (§5.2 prerequisite) |
57	| **E** scaffold-surveyor default | **Needs design first** | Rig template shape (§5.4); mandate priority handoff (§5.5); depends on C + D |
58	
59	### 2.2 What this means in practice
60	
61	- **Dispatch B and A immediately.** Both bounded; both unblock real
62	  patron value (B clears the conceptual landmines, A delivers the
63	  authoring loop standalone). Neither needs more design.
64	- **Resolve the C/D/E gating questions before commissioning the
65	  substrate.** Each is a small design conversation; collectively a
66	  focused session or two before substrate brief is draftable.
67	- **F and G stand alone.** Vision-completion criteria (§5.9) and
68	  walkthrough CLI (§5.11) don't block the substrate but the
69	  substrate's value is incomplete without them. Walkthrough in

# Cartograph + Surveying Cascade — Planning & Commission Synthesis

> Sanctum-side planning artifact. The settled architecture lives in
> the framework repo at
> [`docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md`](/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md).
> This doc holds the planning structure, phased commissions, click
> tree, and source-click traceability. Read the arch doc first if
> you want the design itself.

---

## 1. State of the system

### 1.1 What's shipped

| Component | Package | Key commits | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cartograph data layer (vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs) | `@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus` | `f69f1c6` (scaffold) | **Shipped** |
| Cartograph CLI (vision/charge/piece × create/show/list/patch/transition) | (in cartograph-apparatus) | `a04cd6c` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner core (registry, source-id grammar, ext slot, petition/withdraw helpers) | `@shardworks/reckoner-apparatus` | `89fb1ab`, `64113e0`, `f7fb5b0` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner CDC handler + Reckonings book + startup catch-up | (reckoner) | `e59abd6` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner scheduler kit-contribution (always-approve default) | (reckoner) | `d068b65` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner periodic tick (replaces CDC handler) | (reckoner) | `7bed456` | **Shipped** |
| Reckoner dependency-aware consideration gate | (reckoner) | `0e1e81f` | **Shipped** |
| `depends-on` link kind (Clerk-contributed) | clerk + spider | `5f4a0f1` | **Shipped** |
| Vision-keeper worked-example petitioner | `@shardworks/vision-keeper-apparatus` | `4147d4f`, `b92dc90` | **To delete (Commission B)** |

### 1.2 What's in flight

Nothing currently in flight in this domain.

### 1.3 What hasn't been built

The entire surveying cascade machinery:

- Disk-authored visions (`<GUILD>/vision/<slug>/vision.md` + sidecar)
- `nsg vision apply` CLI verb (and its priority flags)
- Survey writ types (`survey-vision` / `survey-charge` / `survey-piece`)
- SurveyDoc companion type and `books.surveys`
- `ext['surveyor']` slot — priority hints
- Surveyor-apparatus substrate plugin
- Default scaffold-surveyor plugin
- Spider extension: dispatch survey-* writ types in addition to mandates

---

## 2. Commission readiness — what can ship now vs what needs more design

### 2.1 Readiness summary

| Commission | Status | Gating open questions |
|---|---|---|
| **B** Cleanup (delete vision-keeper plugin + redefine The Surveyor) | **Ready now** | None — fully settled |
| **A** Vision authoring on disk + apply CLI | **Ready now** (with v0 leans) | Stage gating (arch §5.1; v0 lean: every apply triggers); sidecar layout (arch §5.13; v0 lean: keep merged) |
| **C** Surveyor-apparatus substrate | **Needs design first** | Dynamic rig templates (arch §5.2); writ type shape 1 vs 3 (§5.3); substrate scope (§5.6); rig failure handling (§5.10); zero-children behavior (§5.14) |
| **D** Spider rig-resolution audit + dispatch extension | **Needs design first** | Spider audit (§5.2 prerequisite) |
| **E** scaffold-surveyor default | **Needs design first** | Rig template shape (§5.4); mandate priority handoff (§5.5); depends on C + D |

### 2.2 What this means in practice

- **Dispatch B and A immediately.** Both bounded; both unblock real
  patron value (B clears the conceptual landmines, A delivers the
  authoring loop standalone). Neither needs more design.
- **Resolve the C/D/E gating questions before commissioning the
  substrate.** Each is a small design conversation; collectively a
  focused session or two before substrate brief is draftable.
- **F and G stand alone.** Vision-completion criteria (§5.9) and
  walkthrough CLI (§5.11) don't block the substrate but the
  substrate's value is incomplete without them. Walkthrough in
  particular is half the patron's experience.

---

## 3. Commission briefs

### 3.1 Ready-now commissions

#### Commission A — Vision authoring on disk

- `nsg vision apply <slug>` CLI verb (single code path for
  first/Nth import)
- Sidecar schema (`vision-metadata.yml` with `visionId`, stage,
  codex, optional `severity`/`deadline`/`decay`)
- CLI flags `--severity`, `--deadline`, `--decay` (override sidecar)
- Writes `ext['surveyor']` to vision writ
- Stale-binding detection (warn at scan, error on apply)
- Tests: import a fresh vision, re-import after edits, error on
  stale binding, priority flag merging

Ships in the cartograph-apparatus package. Note: `ext['surveyor']`
slot is owned by surveyor-apparatus substrate (which doesn't exist
yet in v0). This commission contributes the writes; the substrate
will read them when it lands. For v0 the writes are inert until the
substrate exists.

Single-event-per-apply guarantee: the apply CLI must wrap
create + transition in one Stacks transaction, or
`cartograph.createVision` must accept the initial stage as a
parameter. (Picking which is part of the commission's spec phase.)

#### Commission B — Delete vision-keeper-apparatus + redefine The Surveyor

Sanctum-coordinated framework change:
- Delete `packages/plugins/vision-keeper/` (whole directory)
- Update `guild-metaphor.md`: redefine The Surveyor as the
  cartograph-decomposition apparatus (replacing the codex-mapping
  framing)
- Update `architecture/index.md` and `architecture/plugins.md`:
  remove codex-mapping framing of Surveyor
- Update `petitioner-registration.md`: remove worked-example
  references to the vision-keeper plugin
- Update any test fixtures or `guild.json` files that include
  vision-keeper
- Update the new architecture doc
  (`architecture/surveying-cascade.md`) is already in place; this
  commission completes the cleanup around it

Lands first to clear the path for substrate work later.

### 3.2 Design-pending commissions

These are scoped here for visibility, but should not be drafted as
briefs until their gating questions resolve.

#### Commission C — Surveyor-apparatus substrate

- New plugin `@shardworks/surveyor-apparatus`
- Three survey writ types (`survey-vision`, `survey-charge`,
  `survey-piece`)
- SurveyDoc + `books.surveys`
- Surveyor registry (kit-contribution surface)
- `ext['surveyor']` slot ownership + dimension-translation logic
- CDC observer on cartograph book events
- Rig-template routing (substrate looks up registered surveyor's
  templates)
- SurveyDoc stamping on rig completion
- Surveyor anima tool surface (create_charge[s], create_piece[s],
  create_mandate[s])
- Tests: CDC fires → survey writ created → ext.reckoner stamped per
  hints + defaults → SurveyDoc populated on completion

Depends on resolution of arch §5.2 (dynamic rig-template
registration).

#### Commission D — Spider extension for survey writ dispatch

- Audit Spider's current rig-resolution mechanism (§5.2 prerequisite)
- Extend dispatchable-type set to include survey writ types
- Template lookup integrates with the substrate's surveyor registry
- Tests: a typed survey writ in `open` phase dispatches the
  registered surveyor's rig

May fold into Commission C or stand alone depending on audit scope.

#### Commission E — scaffold-surveyor default

- New plugin `@shardworks/scaffold-surveyor`
- Three rig templates (vision, charge, piece) — basic LLM prompts
- Registers with the substrate via kit contribution
- Tests: end-to-end smoke test (vision applied → survey writ
  created → rig dispatched → charges created → recursion fires →
  mandates created)

Depends on C + D. First-light commission for the whole cascade.

### 3.3 Phase 3 commissions (independent — patron-experience completion)

#### Commission F — Vision completion criteria

Design what "vision complete" means and the CLI/UX for getting
there. Per arch §5.9 — current lean is "patron-explicit, no
auto-completion." Commission turns that into a designed surface.

#### Commission G — Patron walkthrough CLI

The patron-contract validation step at the charge layer. Per arch
§5.11 — substantial design problem. Half of the patron's value lives
here. Independent of substrate work but needed for end-to-end
patron value.

---

## 4. Order of operations

```
[Phase 1 — dispatch immediately]
B (cleanup)        ──→  unblocks substrate naming
A (vision apply)   ──→  delivers authoring loop standalone

[Phase 2 — design conversation, then dispatch]
        ┌───→ C (substrate)
audit ──┤
        └───→ D (Spider extension)
                                │
                                ▼
                         E (default surveyor) ──→ E2E smoke

[Phase 3 — independent of substrate, but completes patron value]
F (vision completion)
G (walkthrough CLI)
```

Phase 1 commissions can dispatch today. Phase 2 needs a design pass
on the gating questions before briefs are drafted. Phase 3 is
independent.

---

## 5. Proposed click tree (replacing c-moa42rxh subtree)

```
c-NEW-ROOT  Cartograph + surveying cascade — patron-vision authoring
            and recursive surveying through to mandate dispatch  [live]
│
├── [Phase 1 — ready to dispatch]
├── c-NEW-A  Vision authoring on disk + apply CLI                 [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-A1  Stage gating policy (arch §5.1)                 [live]
│   └── c-NEW-A2  Sidecar file organization (arch §5.13)          [live]
├── c-NEW-B  Cleanup: delete vision-keeper plugin + redefine      [live]
│            Surveyor in architecture docs
│
├── [Phase 2 — design pending]
├── c-NEW-C  Surveyor-apparatus substrate                         [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-C1  Substrate scope detail (was c-moje49pk)         [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-C2  Survey writ type shape — one or three (§5.3)    [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-C3  Cascading supersedes policy (§5.8)              [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-C4  Survey rig failure handling (§5.10)             [live]
│   └── c-NEW-C5  Zero-children survey behavior (§5.14)           [live]
├── c-NEW-D  Spider rig-resolution audit + dispatch extension     [live]
│   └── c-NEW-D1  Dynamic rig-template registration               [live]
│                  (was c-moje41iq)
├── c-NEW-E  Default scaffold-surveyor                            [live]
│   ├── c-NEW-E1  Rig template shape per layer (was c-moje4dj9)   [live]
│   └── c-NEW-E2  Mandate-creation priority handoff (§5.5)        [live]
│
├── [Phase 3 — independent, patron-experience completion]
├── c-NEW-F  Vision completion criteria (§5.9)                    [live]
├── c-NEW-G  Patron walkthrough CLI surface (§5.11)               [live]
│
├── [Documentation only — no commission needed]
├── c-NEW-H  Re-survey upstream-cascade behavior — document and   [live]
│            confirm; not a code change (§5.15)
│
└── [Parked — future]
    ├── c-NEW-Y  Plural surveyors — future                         [parked]
    └── c-NEW-Z  Per-charge priority nudge CLI — future (§5.12)    [parked]
```

The reorg shifts focus from "design questions about a vision-keeper"
to "commissions and their open sub-questions." Old design questions
either resolved (concluded) or got absorbed into commission scope.

---

## Appendix: source clicks

The clicks this synthesis draws from. Concluded conclusions are the

**Concluded (load-bearing decisions):**
