Produced tiered findings doc at .scratch/clockworks-retrofit-candidates.md identifying Spider's polling block-types (writ-phase / book-updated / animator-paused) as the highest-leverage retrofit, Sentinel's stuck/failed pulses and the sanctum Laboratory's three CDC watchers as clean follow-ons, and Vision-keeper's decline relay (plus a broader README sweep) as the kit-default-standing-orders ergonomics tier. Flagged the Lattice startup pending-scan ↔ Clockworks crash-window relationship as a fold-in for existing click c-mofxqp25, and noted the speculative rig-events click c-mo3ibj94 may be obsolete given the universal writ-status emission already exists. No clicks opened, no commissions dispatched — analysis only, awaiting Sean to pick a thread."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

- session: 21c3e466-2f32-45c5-bfe5-4692f212817e
  date: 2026-04-29
  item: "Surveyed every plugin's use of writ.ext / writ.status against the new sub-slot pattern; identified Cartograph as the highest-leverage cleanup candidate (three companion books — VisionDoc/ChargeDoc/PieceDoc — that hold pure metadata duplicating writ row state). Traced impact on the in-flight surveying-cascade design (c-moji050w subtree): cleanup doesn't break the architecture; it potentially simplifies it (SurveyDoc folds in alongside; CDC observer collapses from three per-book streams to one writ-type-filtered stream). Recommended landing the cleanup before Commission C is briefed against the substrate. Amended c-moji0ggh (Commission C) to drop books.surveys / SurveyDoc-stamping references and add the prerequisite. Updated the synthesis scratch doc with a Phase 0 Commission Z entry. Drafted and dispatched the cleanup commission as w-mojmj0rc-daec01ea159a (codex=nexus, open phase). Monitored to completion (62 min wall-clock; sealed via rebase as commit 607b572). Verified post-completion: implementation grafted ext['cartograph'] over the three companion books with byte-stable typed-API surface; arch doc updates were flagged as patron-grafted because the artificer's draft worktree pre-dated the surveying-cascade arch doc. Read the artificer-produced fragment file (surveying-cascade-fragment.md), grafted §3.4 / §3.6 / §3.7 rewrites + vocabulary table + Phase-1 ASCII flow + Related-documents pointer into the canonical arch doc, deleted the fragment, committed as ab3008c and pushed."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

- session: 031038e6-5487-4813-a565-b36dfaa73cff
  date: 2026-04-29
  item: "Worked c-moje49pk (surveyor-apparatus substrate scope detail). Drafted scope analysis in scratch separating settled boundary statements from open ergonomic boundary questions: priority-dimension defaults (substrate-only — operators reason via one table), source-id grammar (substrate composes <surveyor.id>.<rig-name>), role-file convention (per-rig files owned by impl for v0), tool-catalogue subsetting (substrate-imposed via layer→tools constant), Surveyor kit-contribution interface ({id, description, rigTemplates per-layer} mirroring supportKit.schedulers). Sean introduced the slot-cleanup framing mid-thread; folded shift in (substrate gets thinner — no books.surveys, single CDC subscription on clerk.writs filtered by type) and confirmed the substrate-vs-impl boundary survives unchanged. Monitored Commission Z (rig-mojmj4e5-017314cd) — already terminal at poll start; previous session 21c3e466 had grafted the artificer's fragment as ab3008c. Examined 607b572 + the grafted §3.4 to read the empirical ext/status split: ext['surveyor'] = registration-time provenance ({rigVersion, surveyorId}), status['surveyor'] = completion-time observations; three earlier-spec fields (targetNodeId, rigName, completedAt) drop because Clerk-canonical parentId/type/resolvedAt cover them. Codified the ext-vs-status semantic distinction (ext = data the writ carries; status = substrate observations) as a new Patterns section in docs/architecture/index.md (commit 93f8ce5, pushed). Concluded c-moje49pk with the substrate-vs-impl boundary table and the Patterns-section pointer; deleted the scratch draft."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

input.content:
# Session Summary

Session: 44587ba4-f31b-4fd6-9bd3-945ef870a442
Renamed: free-3 → vision-phase-2

## What we did

- Audited Spider's existing rig-resolution mechanism against the §5.2 framing (`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider.ts`, `types.ts`). Found Spider already supports two-channel registration (config + kit), with kit-channel templates stored under qualified `${pluginId}.${name}` and config-wins-over-kit precedence. Astrolabe is the existing production consumer (`packages/plugins/astrolabe/src/astrolabe.ts:368`).
- Worked through three resolution paths for the substrate-vs-implementation routing boundary: Path A (impls contribute mappings directly), Path B (substrate-as-router via runtime API — withdrawn as infeasible), Path C (substrate-as-router via factory-time config read), and K2 (substrate-as-router via function-form kit, mirroring `clockworks-stacks-signals/events`).
- Concluded c-moje41iq (D1) with the **K3 resolution**: surveyor implementations contribute templates + mappings together; operators mix per-layer via `spider.rigTemplateMappings`. No Spider changes for v0.
- Concluded c-moji0i7s (Commission D parent) — D collapses into documentation; the dispatch story becomes a one-paragraph note in Commission C's brief rather than a standalone framework commission.
- Discovered c-moje49pk (C1, substrate scope detail) had been concluded by a parallel earlier-today session at 12:09 UTC; updated mental model accordingly.

## Decisions made and/or deferred

- **K3 over K2/C1.** Substrate doesn't own routing. The spider.rigTemplateMappings override mechanism already provides per-layer surveyor mixing for operators. The function-form kit pattern (clockworks-stacks-signals precedent) was the most aesthetically clean alternative, but it earns no user-facing capability over the existing mechanism — Sean correctly pushed back: "we would be implementing K2 (or K1) for what gain, specifically?"
- **Commission D collapses to documentation.** No Spider changes; not a standalone commission.
- **Did NOT introduce an explicit "Phase 2" parent click.** Phase 2 is a sequencing concept in the synthesis doc; the commission clicks (C/D/E) sit as siblings under c-moji050w. Implicit grouping deemed sufficient.

## Next steps & open questions

- **Phase 2 confirmation sweep** on the five v0-leaned clicks (C2, C3, C4, C5, E2). All have leans baked into the surveying-cascade arch doc; expect these to be tight confirm-or-refine passes.
- **E1 (`c-moje4dj9`)** — substantive scaffold-surveyor rig design (prompt structure, stop conditions, model selection). Can wait until C lands.
- **Commission C is brief-draftable** once C2–C5 conclude. C1 already settled; D collapsed; routing story is "Astrolabe pattern."
- **Update synthesis doc and arch §5.2** to reflect the K3 resolution — deferred this session, worth doing before drafting C's brief.

## Notable moments

- **Sean's pushback on K2 was the pivot.** *"so we would be implementing K2 (or K1) for what gain, specifically? to allow selection of survey templates in guild json via something like `surveyor.vision-rig` instead of `spider.rigTemplates.survey-vision`?"* — cut through Coco's architectural-purity drift back to user-facing-capability framing. The honest answer was "almost nothing." This is the second time this session that Sean's targeted question reshaped the recommendation; the pattern is a useful counterweight to design-space exploration spiraling.
- **Sean spotted the function-form kit precedent** by pointing to a specific file/line (`clockworks-stacks-signals.ts:183`). Coco had asserted no such pattern existed; it did. Memorable as an example of human-side recall surfacing concrete prior art when the agent missed it.
- **The audit-first instinct paid off.** §5.2's framing ("Spider currently resolves via static rigTemplateMappings") was stale — the dynamic kit-contribution mechanism already exists in production. Reading the code first turned an open design question into a one-paragraph documentation note.

## Experiment observations

None directly tied to active experiments. The session is relevant to the broader X008 (criteria-internalization) narrative as an instance of patron-anima collaboration where the human's targeted questions repeatedly rerouted the agent away from over-elaboration.

content.text:
Committed (`1195c448`) and pushed.

---

# Session Summary

## What we did

- Audited Spider's rig-resolution mechanism (`packages/plugins/spider/src/spider.ts`, `types.ts`). Found Spider already supports two-channel registration with config-wins-over-kit precedence; Astrolabe is the existing production consumer of the kit channel.
- Walked four resolution paths (A/B/C/K2) for substrate-vs-implementation routing boundary.
- Concluded **c-moje41iq** (D1) with the **K3 resolution**: surveyor implementations contribute templates + mappings together via Spider's existing kit channel; operators mix per-layer via `spider.rigTemplateMappings` config.
- Concluded **c-moji0i7s** (Commission D parent) — Commission D collapses to a documentation note inside Commission C's brief.
- Discovered c-moje49pk (C1) had been concluded by a parallel earlier-today session.

## Decisions made and/or deferred

- **K3 over K2/C1.** Substrate doesn't own routing.
- **Commission D collapses to documentation.** No standalone framework commission.
- **No explicit "Phase 2" parent click** introduced — the implicit grouping under c-moji050w's commission siblings is sufficient.
- **Deferred:** updating `.scratch/cartograph-design-synthesis.md` and arch §5.2 to reflect the K3 resolution.

## Next steps & open questions

- **Confirmation sweep** on C2/C3/C4/C5/E2 (five v0-leaned clicks) — should land tightly in one focused pass.
- **E1 (`c-moje4dj9`)** — substantive scaffold-surveyor rig design (prompt structure, stop conditions). Can wait until C lands.
- **Update synthesis doc and arch §5.2** to reflect K3 resolution before drafting C's brief.

## Notable moments

- **Sean's pushback on K2** — *"so we would be implementing K2 (or K1) for what gain, specifically?"* — cut through Coco's architectural-purity drift and forced a user-facing-capability framing. The honest answer was "almost nothing." Notable as a recurring corrective pattern this session.
- **Sean spotted the function-form kit precedent** by file/line (`clockworks-stacks-signals.ts:183`) after Coco initially asserted no such pattern existed. Concrete prior art surfaced by human-side recall.
- **The audit-first instinct paid off.** §5.2's framing was stale; reading code first turned an open design question into a one-paragraph documentation note.

## Reminders

🐒 Hey Sean — your last ethnographer interview was 2026-04-23 (six days ago). Might be worth a check-in soon if you have the time.
